sourceSummary.oneLine: Device description for the HnX-P1 and HnX-PB battery-powered portable X-ray systems, including classification, intended use, specifications, warnings, and manufacturing/QC process.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory/device-file documentation by describing the portable X-ray system design, models, components, operating principles, specifications, safety warnings, and applicable regulatory classification information.
keyPoints: Trade(Model) Name: HnX-P1, HnX-PB; Device common Name: Portable X-ray System.
Product Code: ILZ; Regulation Number: 21 CFR 892.1720 (Mobile x-ray system); Device class: Class ll.
The portable x-ray system may be used for diagnostic imaging of body extremities and is not for mammography use.
HnX-PB changes only the exterior design compared to HnX-P1; interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, software, etc. are all the same.
The device is battery-powered, designed for handheld or stand-mounted imaging, and may use a film-cassette or flat-panel detector.

entities.name: Portable X-ray System
entities.evidence: Device common Name: Portable X-ray System

entities.name: H&abyz
entities.evidence: This device is a battery-powered portable X-ray System, designed and manufactured by H&abyz.

concepts.name: Portable X-ray System
concepts.evidence: Device common Name: Portable X-ray System

concepts.name: Diagnostic imaging of body extremities
concepts.evidence: The portable x-ray system may be used for diagnostic imaging of body extremities.

concepts.name: Radiation safety
concepts.pageTarget: Radiation Safety for Portable X-ray Systems
concepts.evidence: X-ray produced in device exposure has the hazard of ionizing radiation to human tissues.

concepts.name: APR mode
concepts.evidence: APR(Anatomic Programming Radiography) mode icon Icon indicating saved APR(1~10) setting value.

concepts.name: Bluetooth preview mode
concepts.evidence: A thumbnail image is transmitted from the PC to the “3. Control Unit” using short-range wireless communication (Bluetooth), and the “3. Control Unit” displays the received image on the screen.

evidence.claim: The document describes HnX-P1 and HnX-PB as trade/model names for a Portable X-ray System.
evidence.evidenceText: Trade(Model) Name: HnX-P1, HnX-PB; Device common Name: Portable X-ray System

evidence.claim: The device has FDA product code ILZ and is associated with 21 CFR 892.1720 for mobile x-ray systems.
evidence.evidenceText: Product Code: ILZ; Regulation Number: 21 CFR 892.1720 (Mobile x-ray system)

evidence.claim: The device class is Class ll.
evidence.pageTarget: HnX Portable X-ray System Regulatory Classification

evidence.claim: The device is a battery-powered portable X-ray System designed and manufactured by H&abyz.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is a battery-powered portable X-ray System, designed and manufactured by H&abyz.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB variant differs from HnX-P1 only in exterior design while interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, and software are the same.
evidence.evidenceText: The variant model HnX-PB has only changed the exterior design compared to the existing model HnX-P1, and the interface, exterior material, internal structure, PCB, software, etc. are all the same.

evidence.claim: The portable x-ray system may be used for diagnostic imaging of body extremities.
evidence.evidenceText: The portable x-ray system may be used for diagnostic imaging of body extremities.

evidence.claim: The device is not for mammography use and is not intended to replace a stationary radiographic system.
evidence.evidenceText: - Not for mammography use - This device is not intended to replace a stationary radiographic system, which may be required for full optimization of image quality and radiation exposure for different exam types.

evidence.claim: The device is designed for radiographic uses only and not intended for fluoroscopic use or other associated applications.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is designed for radiographic uses only and not intended for fluoroscopic use or other associated applications.

evidence.claim: The device does not apply to mammography and dental radiography.
evidence.evidenceText: The device does not apply to mammography and dental radiography.

evidence.claim: The HnX-P1 should be used for diagnosis and is not intended for therapy.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-P1 should be used for the diagnosis purpose and is not intended for therapy of any kind.

evidence.claim: The device is specified as Class I and IPX0 equipment under EN 60601-1 standards.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is specified as Class I and IPX0 equipment under EN 60601-1 standards.

evidence.claim: The X-ray generator is a 60 kHz inverter type with tube voltage 50-90 kV.
evidence.evidenceText: X-Ray generator (1) Type: 60 kHz Inverter Type (2) Tube voltage: 50 - 90 kV

evidence.claim: The X-ray tube model is OX/90 with 0.5 mm x 0.5 mm focal spot and 19 degree target angle.
evidence.evidenceText: X-Ray TUBE OX/90 Focal Spot 0.5 mm x 0.5 mm Target Angle 19 degree

evidence.claim: The software names are HnX_P1 and HnX_PB with version format Ver. 00.xx.
HnX_P1 and HnX_PB software differ only by product model name displayed on the LCD screen.
evidence.evidenceText: The only difference between the HnX_P1 and HnX_PB software is the product model name displayed on the LCD screen ("HnX-P1" or "HnX-PB"). All other aspects of the software are identical.

evidence.claim: Software and firmware updates cannot be performed directly by users in the field and must be performed by authorized service personnel or by return to manufacturer or service center.
evidence.evidenceText: the product's software and firmware updates cannot be performed directly by users in the field, and must be performed by authorized service personnel or by returning the product to the manufacturer or service center.

evidence.claim: If a cybersecurity threat is detected, the user should disconnect Bluetooth, turn off the device, and contact the H&abyz service team.
evidence.evidenceText: If a cybersecurity threat is detected, please disconnect the Bluetooth connection first, turn off the device, and then contact the H&abyz service team.

evidence.claim: During X-ray irradiation, the operator shall stand at least 1.8 meters from the patient and X-ray tube and outside the useful beam.
evidence.evidenceText: When X-ray is being irradiated, the operator shall be stand at least 1.8meters(6feet) from the patient, the X-ray tube, and out of the useful beam.
evidence.pageTarget: Radiation Safety for Portable X-ray Systems

evidence.claim: Preparation before use instructs the operator to wear a lead apron and leave at least 2 meters between the operator and the unit.
evidence.evidenceText: Always wear a lead apron during X-ray exposures. Please leave at least 2 meters (≈ 6.5 feet) between the operator and the unit.

reviewFlags.reason: Document date is not present in the provided source text or metadata.
Revision/version is taken from the sourcePath filename '(Rev3)' rather than a revision field in the document body.
Authority is indicated by source metadata authoritySignal 'FDA' and FDA folder path; the document body provides product code ILZ and 21 CFR 892.1720 but does not explicitly name FDA.
Source text contains repeated spelling/casing inconsistencies such as 'Potable X-ray System', 'Hnx-PB', and 'Class ll'.
